questions.questionText: What may be the reason that friends are similar to each other according to the recent research?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据最近的研究，朋友之间相似的原因可能是什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Their brains work in harmony.

options.label: B
options.text: They are generally the same age.

options.label: C
options.text: Their interests are quite similar.

options.label: D
options.text: They have the same ethnic background.

questions.questionText: What does the news report say about the relationship between friends?
questions.questionTextZh: 新闻报道对朋友之间的关系说了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It can be touching.

options.label: B
options.text: It is hard to predict.

options.label: C
options.text: It can work both ways.

options.label: D
options.text: It resembles family ties.

questions.questionText: What did both Alan Robinson and Walter McDonald attempt to do on DNA matching websites?
questions.questionTextZh: Alan Robinson和Walter McDonald在DNA匹配网站上尝试做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Search for their half-brothers.

options.label: B
options.text: Identify their biological fathers.

options.label: C
options.text: Find out more about their ancestry.

options.label: D
options.text: See whether they are actually related.

questions.questionText: What did the news report say about Alan Robinson and Walter McDonald?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于Alan Robinson和Walter McDonald，新闻报道说了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: They were both given up for adoption.

options.label: B
options.text: They were born to the same mother.

options.label: C
options.text: They flew 737 airplanes as pilots.

options.label: D
options.text: They were both 60 years of age.

questions.questionText: Why did one of the male tourists feel sad?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么其中一位男性游客感到难过？

options.label: A
options.text: One of his friends was caught littering.

options.label: B
options.text: Other tourists refused to join in the cleanup.

options.label: C
options.text: The beautiful beach was spoiled with lots of trash.

options.label: D
options.text: The kilometer-long beach was practically deserted.

questions.questionText: Who put the good deed video on the internet?
questions.questionTextZh: 谁把善举视频放到了网上？

options.label: A
options.text: A passerby.

options.label: B
options.text: A local woman.

options.label: C
options.text: The beach authorities.

options.label: D
options.text: One of the five tourists.

questions.questionText: Why did one local woman say she was embarrassed?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么一位当地女性说她感到尴尬？

options.label: A
options.text: The tourists' good deed was not noticed by the locals.

options.label: B
options.text: Some natives were selling poor-quality food to tourists.

options.label: C
options.text: The number of tourists to the beach is on a steady decline.

options.label: D
options.text: It was tourists not natives who were cleaning up the beach.

questions.questionText: Why can't the man go for a run Saturday morning?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么男士周六早上不能去跑步？

options.label: A
options.text: He has to play football with workmates.

options.label: B
options.text: He has got some books to read.

options.label: C
options.text: He is going to visit a friend.

options.label: D
options.text: He is physically unfit for it.

questions.questionText: Why is the woman's whole family going to the zoo next Saturday?
questions.questionTextZh: 为什么女士全家下周六要去动物园？

options.label: A
options.text: To teach kids about animal protection.

options.label: B
options.text: To learn how popular zoos could be.

options.label: C
options.text: To see some rare animals in cages.

options.label: D
options.text: To give her little nephew a treat.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about Dan from the man's description?
questions.questionTextZh: 从男士的描述中，我们了解到关于Dan的什么信息？

options.label: A
options.text: He enjoys excellent health.

options.label: B
options.text: He is keen on extreme sports.

options.label: C
options.text: He coaches tennis players every week.

options.label: D
options.text: He spends most of his time in the gym.

questions.questionText: What has the man been doing since last weekend?
questions.questionTextZh: 自上周末以来男士一直在做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Tending to his swollen ankle.

options.label: B
options.text: Concentrating on reading.

options.label: C
options.text: Writing three book reports.

options.label: D
options.text: Planning Christmas celebrations.

questions.questionText: What do we learn about the man's book published recently?
questions.questionTextZh: 关于男士最近出版的书，我们了解到什么？

options.label: A
options.text: It is being debated by hundreds of retirees.

options.label: B
options.text: It is attracting many people's attention.

options.label: C
options.text: It partly records his own experience.

options.label: D
options.text: It argues for postponing retirement.

questions.questionText: What do the articles the woman read recommend?
questions.questionTextZh: 女士读到的文章推荐什么？

options.label: A
options.text: One should foresee a financial crisis.

options.label: B
options.text: One should trust financial planners' figures.

options.label: C
options.text: One should have one million dollars to retire.

options.label: D
options.text: One should start saving as early as possible.

questions.questionText: What does the man say about retirement?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士对退休有什么看法？

options.label: A
options.text: It doesn't need to be permanent.

options.label: B
options.text: It shouldn't be considered risky.

options.label: C
options.text: It helps to reduce travel expenses.

options.label: D
options.text: It is the way to quit a job one hates.

questions.questionText: How does the man say his proposal about retirement can be carried out?
questions.questionTextZh: 男士说他的退休建议如何实现？

options.label: A
options.text: By keeping close contact with one's employers.

options.label: B
options.text: By retiring when one reaches sixty years old.

options.label: C
options.text: By investing half of one's monthly income.

options.label: D
options.text: By following the counsel of financial planners.

questions.questionText: What was the finding of the new research from America about consumers' food choices?
questions.questionTextZh: 美国关于消费者食物选择的新研究发现了什么？

options.label: A
options.text: They tended to be easily anticipated by those belonging to their own race.

options.label: B
options.text: They tended to be arbitrarily judged by individuals of opposing groups.

options.label: C
options.text: They were readily shared among members of the same social or racial group.

options.label: D
options.text: They were influenced by the presence of someone from an outsider group.

questions.questionText: When did 31 percent of students in the experiment select the healthier option?
questions.questionTextZh: 实验中31%的学生什么时候选择了更健康的选项？

options.label: A
options.text: When they tried to make a positive impression on the researchers.

options.label: B
options.text: When an unknown student from another university was present.

options.label: C
options.text: When an experimenter from the research team took notice.

options.label: D
options.text: When they were offered both candy and fruit as a snack.

questions.questionText: How could a healthy diet be promoted according to the findings of the new research?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据新研究的发现，如何促进健康饮食？

options.label: A
options.text: By advertising its social benefits.

options.label: B
options.text: By teaching consumers diet strategies.

options.label: C
options.text: By supporting struggling consumers.

options.label: D
options.text: By maintaining its positive image.

questions.questionText: What does the recent study led by Patricia Pendry aim to examine?
questions.questionTextZh: Patricia Pendry领导的近期研究旨在考察什么？

options.label: A
options.text: The academic and learning issues struggling students encounter.

options.label: B
options.text: The risk students face due to a history of mental health problems.

options.label: C
options.text: The effect of interacting with therapy dogs on students under pressure.

options.label: D
options.text: The work universities are doing to help students succeed academically.

questions.questionText: What did the new study measure in the participants?
questions.questionTextZh: 新研究测量了参与者的什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Their communicative skills.

options.label: B
options.text: Their executive functioning.

options.label: C
options.text: Their academic networking.

options.label: D
options.text: Their leadership capacities.

questions.questionText: What does Patricia Pendry think traditional stress management programs may do?
questions.questionTextZh: Patricia Pendry认为传统的压力管理项目可能做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Rid students of their anxiety.

options.label: B
options.text: Add to some students' stress.

options.label: C
options.text: Contribute little to typical students' success.

options.label: D
options.text: Help students with mental issues pull through.

questions.questionText: What do entrepreneurs have to do when taking risks according to the passage?
questions.questionTextZh: 根据文章，企业家在承担风险时必须做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Work hard and plan carefully.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to succeed at any cost.

options.label: C
options.text: Aim high and expect great results.

options.label: D
options.text: Remain optimistic even in difficulty.

questions.questionText: What does the passage say is key to business growth?
questions.questionTextZh: 文章说什么是业务增长的关键？

options.label: A
options.text: Regarding failure as something inevitable.

options.label: B
options.text: Trying out innovative marketing strategies.

options.label: C
options.text: Venturing into sectors never explored before.

options.label: D
options.text: Being willing to experiment with novel ideas.

questions.questionText: What are we advised to do when we fail?
questions.questionTextZh: 当我们失败时，我们被建议做什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Expect future success so as to move forward.

options.label: B
options.text: Learn from our failure and forge ahead.

options.label: C
options.text: Distinguish between good and bad risks.

options.label: D
options.text: Examine our strategies and find out weaknesses.

questions.questionText: What does it mean to risk-taking businesses when most people tend to avoid risk?
questions.questionTextZh: 当大多数人倾向于规避风险时，这对敢于冒险的企业意味着什么？

options.label: A
options.text: Fresher offers.

options.label: B
options.text: Safer operation.

options.label: C
options.text: More challenges.

options.label: D
options.text: Less competition.
